title: NOC 22232 – Occupational Health and Safety Specialists | GTR Immigration
description: Occupational Health and Safety Specialists under NOC 22232 are critical professionals responsible for ensuring workplace safety, regulatory compliance, and risk mitigation across industries in Canada. As workplace regulations continue to evolve and enforcement standards strengthen nationwide, demand for qualified safety specialists remains strong under this TEER 2 occupation. For internationally trained professionals, NOC 22232 offers competitive eligibility under Express Entry and multiple Provincial Nominee Programs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupationsTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group22 – Technical occupations related to natural and applied sciencesSub-Major Group222 – Technical occupations related to applied sciences (except engineering)Minor Group2223 – Technical inspectors and regulatory officersNOC Code22232

common_job_titles: Safety supervisor – occupational health and safety
duties: Occupational Health and Safety Specialists perform some or all of the following:
Conduct workplace audits and accident investigations
Perform inspections to identify safety hazards
Develop and implement occupational health and safety programs
Draft policies and standard operating procedures
Enforce compliance with federal and provincial safety regulations
Initiate enforcement procedures when standards are violated
Advise employers and employees on legislative compliance
Promote a positive occupational health and safety culture
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in occupational health and safety or related field
Experience in safety auditing or compliance may be required
Certification through the Board of Canadian Registered Safety Professionals (e.g., CRSP designation)
While certification is not always mandatory, it significantly enhances employment prospects.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $42,900 to $109,700, depending on industry, certification, and region.
job_outlook: Certified professionals with CRSP designation may experience stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Occupational Health and Safety Specialists are employed in:

Strong demand exists in industrial and resource-driven provinces such as Alberta, Ontario, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Quebec, as well as northern regions like Nunavut and Northwest Territories.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 22232?
Occupational Health and Safety Specialists benefit from:
Strong job security due to regulatory requirements
Opportunities in both public and private sectors
Clear career progression into senior compliance or safety management roles
Workplace safety is legally mandated in Canada, ensuring sustained need for qualified professionals.
